question:
Each of the following characteristics represent a Kingdom proposed by Whittaker. Arrange the following in increasing order of complexity of body organization.
A. Multicellular heterotrophs with cell wall made of chitin.
B. Heterotrophs with tissue/organ/organ system level of body organization.
C. Prokaryotes with cell wall made of polysaccharides and amino acids.
D. Eukaryotic autotrophs with tissue/organ level of body organization.
E. Eukaryotes with cellular body organization.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
explanation:
The correct answer is option 3.
According to increasing complexity of body organization as per Whittaker’s Five Kingdom classification, the correct sequence is:
C. Monera – Prokaryotes with a cell wall made of polysaccharides and amino acids (least complex)
$ \downarrow $
E. Protista – Unicellular eukaryotes with cellular level of organization
$ \downarrow $
A. Fungi – Multicellular heterotrophs with a chitin cell wall
$ \downarrow $
D. Plantae – Eukaryotic autotrophs with tissue/organ level of organization
$ \downarrow $
B. Animalia – Eukaryotic heterotrophs with tissue/organ/organ system level (most complex) Thus, the increasing order of complexity is C, E, A, D, B.
subject: Biology

question:
The characteristics mentioned below belong to:
(I) No asexual spores are generally formed.
(II) Mycelium is branched and septate.
(III) Vegetative reproduction by fragmentation is common.
(IV) Sex organs are absent, but sexual reproduction takes place by somatogamy.
(V) Basidia are arranged in basidiocarp.
explanation: Club fungi/basidiomycetes show the following features. The mycelium is branched and septate. The asexual spores are generally not found, but vegetative reproduction by fragmentation is common. The sex organs are absent, but plasmogamy is brought about by fusion of two vegetative or somatic cells of different strains or genotypes. The resultant structure is dikaryotic which ultimately gives rise to basidium. Karyogamy and meiosis take place in the basidium producing four basidiospores. The basidiospores are exogenously produced on the basidium.
subject: Biology

explanation: During the sexual reproduction in fungi, two compatible haploid hyphae fuse, in members of Ascomycetes and Basidiomycetes, after fusion of incompatible hyphae (Plasmogamy) the nucleus do not fuse immediately. Cell wall of fungi is made up of chitin and it is a homopolysachharide which includes N-acetyl glucosamine.
